topic: Two strangers realize they've been dating the same person
raw_output:
[story 1]

The coffee shop was overwhelmingly loud, a chaotic symphony of clattering mugs and overlapping conversations. I was nursing a lukewarm latte, trying to focus on the textbook spread across the small table, when a familiar silhouette appeared at the next table. My breath hitched. It was him. The exact way he tilted his head when he was deep in thought, the slight crinkle beside his eyes when he smiled—it was all impossibly precise. My hand froze halfway to turning a page. He looked up, his eyes scanning the room, and for a split second, they locked onto mine. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, a sudden, sharp need to disappear. He gave the slightest, almost imperceptible nod, a gesture that felt like a confirmation of a shared, secret history I couldn't access. I quickly dropped my gaze to my book, my fingers trembling slightly as I tried to steady my hand. The realization washed over me, heavy and suffocating: I wasn't just seeing someone new; I was sharing a life with a ghost.

[story 2]

The evening air was crisp, carrying the scent of damp leaves. I was walking home, the streetlights casting long, distorted shadows, when I bumped into someone. The collision jarred me, and I looked up, my heart lurching against my ribs. Standing there, illuminated by a passing car, was a man whose features were etched into my memory with painful clarity. His smile, that specific, crooked curve of his lips, hit me like a physical blow. I stopped dead in my tracks, my feet feeling rooted to the pavement. He offered a greeting, his voice warm and familiar, and in that moment, the world tilted. Every shared glance, every inside joke, every quiet understanding—it all slammed into me simultaneously. My palms grew slick. I managed a weak, "Hello," the sound barely escaping my throat, desperately trying to pull myself back into the ordinary flow of the night, but the echo of recognition was deafening.

[story 3]

The gallery opening was a swirl of expensive fabric and hushed commentary. I was observing a piece of abstract sculpture, my attention snagged by a figure standing near the entrance. It was him. Unmistakable. The way he adjusted his jacket, the subtle tension in his shoulders—it was the same posture I’d memorized months ago. My vision tunneled. I felt a distinct pressure building behind my eyes, a tightening sensation that made focusing on the art impossible. When his gaze swept across the room and landed on mine, a strange, dizzying recognition sparked between us. He offered a small, tentative smile, a gesture that felt both comforting and utterly devastating. I took an involuntary step backward, my muscles locking up, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole. The silence between us stretched, thick and unbearable, as I wrestled with the undeniable truth blooming in my chest.
